title: How the action graph and action keys work
description: Bazel builds a graph of actions keyed by their inputs, command, and environment, and reuses a cached output whenever the key matches.
body: Use when reasoning about what triggers a rebuild or a cache hit. Do not assume Bazel rebuilds based on file timestamps. Each action's key is a hash of its declared inputs, command line, and relevant environment; a matching key returns the cached output, while any change to those produces a new key and re-execution. Inspect with the execution log to see action keys. Validation signals include a no-op rebuild hitting cache and a real input change producing a new key. Risk boundary: undeclared inputs are absent from the key, so the cache can return stale results, making hermeticity essential.
